---
title: "Menu theme"
description: "Pick a visual theme for your menu — colors, fonts, and dark or light mode."
sidebarTitle: "Theme"
icon: "palette"
---

Every menu has a **visual theme** (colors and fonts) and a **default color
mode** (light, dark, or follow the diner's device). The two are
independent — every theme ships with both light and dark palettes.

## Pick a theme

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open Basics">
    In the menu editor, swipe to the **Basics** tab.
  </Step>
  <Step title="Pick from the theme grid">
    The **Visual theme** picker shows ten curated presets: *Classic*,
    *Minimal*, *Noir*, *Forest*, *Ocean*, *Rosewood*, *Saffron*, *Slate*,
    *Espresso*, *Neon*. Tap one to select.
  </Step>
  <Step title="Save">
    Changes save with the rest of the Basics tab. Diners see the new
    theme on the next page load.
  </Step>
</Steps>

## Default color mode

Under the theme picker, pick how the menu starts:

- **System** — follows the diner's device setting (dark at night, light
  during the day).
- **Light** — always starts in light mode.
- **Dark** — always starts in dark mode.

Diners can override the starting choice with the theme toggle in the menu
header. Their choice is remembered per venue on their device.

## Currency

The Basics tab is also where you set your venue's **currency** (ISO code
— USD, EUR, IRR, and so on). It applies across all your menus. Changes
don't touch the numeric price you entered; they just change what symbol
diners see next to it.
